mission.title: external-secrets: ExternalSecret that refer ECRAuthorizationToken not work in 0.18.0
mission.description: ExternalSecret that refer ECRAuthorizationToken not work in 0.18.0. This issue affects 12+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
After we upgrade the external-secrets version to 0.18.0, the externalsecret that refer the ECRAuthorizationToken report the error, but it works with version 0.17.0(refer the manifest below, it works).

steps.title: Apply the fix for ExternalSecret that refer ECRAuthorizationToken not work in…
steps.description:
Your problem seems to be something with how you’re injecting the manifests than this bug. The live manifests should contain the templates; the fact they don’t have them means you’re probably using a helm renderer 🙂, which is unrelated
```yaml
apiVersion: generators.external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: ECRAuthorizationToken
metadata:
  name: ecr-auth-token
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  region: ap-southeast-2
```

steps.title: Confirm ExternalSecret that refer ECRAuthorizationToken… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Your problem seems to be something with how you’re injecting the manifests than this bug. The live manifests should contain the templates; the fact they don’t have them means you’re probably using a helm renderer 🙂, which is unrelated
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: generators.external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
kind: ECRAuthorizationToken
metadata:
  name: ecr-auth-token
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  region: ap-southeast-2

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: repository-ecr
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  dataFrom:
  - sourceRef:
      generatorRef:
        apiVersion: generators.external-secrets.io/v1alpha1
        kind: ECRAuthorizationToken
        name: ecr-auth-token
  refreshInterval: 30m
  target:
    creationPolicy: Owner
    deletionPolicy: Retain
    template:
      data:
        enableOCI: "true"
        password: "{{ .password }}"
        type: helm
        url: 11111111111.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-2.amazonaws.com
        username: "{{ .username }}"
      engineVersion: v2
      mergePolicy: Replace
      metadata:
        labels:
          argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repo-creds
      type: Opaque

env:
    - name: AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS
      value: regional
    - name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
      value: ap-southeast-2
    - name: AWS_REGION
      value: ap-southeast-2
    - name: AWS_ROLE_ARN
      value: arn:aws:iam::{accountId}:role/{role-name}
    - name: AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE
      value: /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount/token

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
